description:
You are an incident responder who has just received a sample
    authentication log located at:

        /home/user/incident_logs/auth.log

    Your goal is to use nothing more than the standard shell, together
    with **awk** and **sed**, to produce two deliverables that will be
    inspected by an automated grading suite.

    1. A CSV-style summary of failed log-in attempts per source IP:
          /home/user/analysis/failed_login_report.csv

    2. A redacted copy of only the “Failed password …” lines, with the
       TCP port number scrubbed, saved as:
          /home/user/analysis/sanitized_failed.log


    A.  failed_login_report.csv
    ---------------------------------
    • Create the directory /home/user/analysis/ if it is not present.
    • Extract only the log lines that contain the literal text
      “Failed password”.
    • From those lines pull out the IPv4 address that appears after
      the word “from”.
    • Count the number of occurrences of each distinct IP.
    • Sort the table:
         1. Primary key  – descending numerical count  
         2. Secondary key – ascending, lexicographic IP
    • Write the results in **exactly** the format shown below.  
      The file MUST end with a single trailing newline.

        ip_address,attempts
        <top_IP>,<count>
        <next_IP>,<count>
        …
        TOTAL_UNIQUE_IPS:<N>

      Example of correct formatting (values will differ):

        ip_address,attempts
        203.0.113.45,7
        198.51.100.23,4
        TOTAL_UNIQUE_IPS:2

      Notes  
      • There must be NO spaces before or after commas or colons.  
      • The header line is always “ip_address,attempts”.  
      • The final line begins with “TOTAL_UNIQUE_IPS:” immediately
        followed by the number of unique attacker IPs found.


    B.  sanitized_failed.log
    ---------------------------------
    • Start with the same set of “Failed password …” lines.
    • Replace the port number (the integer appearing right after
      the word “port”) with the literal string “XXXX”.  
      Example transformation:

          “… from 192.0.2.77 port 54324 ssh2”
          becomes
          “… from 192.0.2.77 port XXXX ssh2”

    • Preserve the rest of each line exactly (date, PID, user name,
      spacing, etc.).
    • Keep the lines in their original chronological order.
    • The file must also end with a single trailing newline.


    Constraints & Hints
    ---------------------------------
    1. You should be able to accomplish the task with three or four
       reasonably short shell commands in total.
    2. Only **awk**, **sed**, pipes, redirection, and mkdir are needed.
    3. Do NOT delete or modify the original auth.log file.
    4. After creating the two files, display their contents to the
       terminal so a human reviewer can immediately inspect them.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for the grading harness.
        The auth.log file is pre-populated with the lines below and
        will not change between runs.  The agent must NOT see these
        contents here; they are used solely for automated verification.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/incident_logs/auth.log"><![CDATA[
# Authentication log file sample
# Generated on 2023-09-25

Sep 25 12:15:01 server1 sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port 54321 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:05 server1 sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 198.51.100.23 port 54322 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:10 server1 sshd[12347]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port 54323 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:15 server1 sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 192.0.2.77 port 54324 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:20 server1 sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port 54325 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:26 server1 sshd[12350]: Accepted password for validuser from 10.0.0.1 port 54326 ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:30 server1 sshd[12351]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 198.51.100.23 port 54327 ssh2
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/failed_login_report.csv"><![CDATA[
ip_address,attempts
203.0.113.45,3
198.51.100.23,2
192.0.2.77,1
TOTAL_UNIQUE_IPS:3
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis/sanitized_failed.log"><![CDATA[
Sep 25 12:15:01 server1 sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port XXXX ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:05 server1 sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 198.51.100.23 port XXXX ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:10 server1 sshd[12347]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port XXXX ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:15 server1 sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 192.0.2.77 port XXXX ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:20 server1 sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port XXXX ssh2
Sep 25 12:15:30 server1 sshd[12351]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 198.51.100.23 port XXXX ssh2
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
name: task_000000_b46eb623
